title: orchis lactea subsp. broteroana
intro: Orchis lactea subsp. broteroana is a sub-species of the orchid plant, specifically a type of wild orchid found in the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is known to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and efficacy may require further research and validation.
